attributes.description: Alerts when `sqlserver.lock.timeout.rate > 0`. Queries giving up waiting for locks indicate contention beyond clients' patience.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Lock timeouts

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server lock timeouts

### What fired
Lock timeout rate exceeded 0 during the window.

### Why it matters
Timeouts mean queries failed rather than waited — worse user experience than blocking alone. Usually paired with rising lock waits and blocked processes.

### Triage
1. Look at blocked processes and lock-wait rate on the same host.
2. Check application-side lock timeout settings.

### Remediation
- Reduce transaction hold time.
- Apply row-versioning / snapshot isolation where safe.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero rate.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Lock timeouts: queries giving up waiting for locks
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.lock.timeout.rate IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_timeouts = MAX(metrics.sqlserver.lock.timeout.rate),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
| WHERE max_timeouts > 0
| KEEP instance_id, max_timeouts
